questions.stem: This electron micrograph shows a rod-shaped bacterium bearing a single appendage extending from one pole. What structure is this and what function does it provide?

choices.label: A
choices.text: An axial filament for corkscrew motion

choices.label: B
choices.text: A fimbria used only for adhesion

choices.label: D
choices.text: A single polar flagellum providing motility

explanation.summary: Pseudomonas spp. are motile by a single polar flagellum, which drives their characteristic swimming motility and helps them reach and colonize surfaces.
explanation.whyCorrect: Pseudomonas spp. are motile by a single polar flagellum, which drives their characteristic swimming motility and helps them reach and colonize surfaces.

questions.stem: Colonies growing on this agar plate produce a diffusing blue-green discoloration of the medium. Which pigment is chiefly responsible for this color and is directly toxic to host cells?

explanation.summary: Pyocyanin is a blue pigment produced by P. aeruginosa that is toxic to cells including leukocytes and epithelial cells, and together with yellow pyoverdine gives colonies their blue-to-aqua color.
explanation.whyCorrect: Pyocyanin is a blue pigment produced by P. aeruginosa that is toxic to cells including leukocytes and epithelial cells, and together with yellow pyoverdine gives colonies their blue-to-aqua color.

questions.stem: This agar plate shows bacterial growth with a diffuse aqua-green discoloration spreading through the medium. This colony appearance and pigmentation is most characteristic of which organism?

explanation.summary: Pseudomonas colonies characteristically show an aqua-to-green color from pyocyanin and pyoverdine; it is a non-lactose-fermenting, oxidase-positive gram-negative rod.
explanation.whyCorrect: Pseudomonas colonies characteristically show an aqua-to-green color from pyocyanin and pyoverdine; it is a non-lactose-fermenting, oxidase-positive gram-negative rod.

questions.stem: This split plate compares two strains of the same organism, one dry and one glistening and slimy. Overproduction of which substance accounts for the slimy (mucoid) phenotype on the right?

explanation.summary: Mucoid strains, characteristic of cystic fibrosis isolates, overproduce an alginate capsule that can absorb hundreds of times its weight in water and helps the bacteria adhere to and colonize mucus.
explanation.whyCorrect: Mucoid strains, characteristic of cystic fibrosis isolates, overproduce an alginate capsule that can absorb hundreds of times its weight in water and helps the bacteria adhere to and colonize mucus.

questions.stem: This Gram stain from a ventilator-associated pneumonia isolate shows pink-staining organisms that are short and plump rather than long rods. What is the shape and Gram reaction depicted?

explanation.summary: Acinetobacter spp. appear as gram-negative coccobacilli (a shape between a sphere and a rod), are non-motile and oxidase-negative, and are a common cause of ventilator-associated pneumonia.
explanation.whyCorrect: Acinetobacter spp. appear as gram-negative coccobacilli (a shape between a sphere and a rod), are non-motile and oxidase-negative, and are a common cause of ventilator-associated pneumonia.

questions.stem: A ventilated ICU patient develops the eye finding shown, with a corneal infiltrate and surrounding conjunctival injection. Which organism is the most likely cause of this rapidly progressive keratitis?

explanation.summary: Pseudomonas aeruginosa is a leading cause of severe, rapidly progressive bacterial keratitis, especially with contact lens use or contaminated solutions; its proteases and toxins drive aggressive corneal destruction.
explanation.whyCorrect: Pseudomonas aeruginosa is a leading cause of severe, rapidly progressive bacterial keratitis, especially with contact lens use or contaminated solutions; its proteases and toxins drive aggressive corneal destruction.

questions.stem: A patient develops the itchy, tender red papules and pustules shown over the trunk two days after using a poorly maintained hot tub. Which organism is the most likely cause?

explanation.summary: Hot tub folliculitis is caused by Pseudomonas contaminating warm water; the bacteria enter hair follicles through microabrasions, producing follicular papules and pustules that usually resolve without complications.
explanation.whyCorrect: Hot tub folliculitis is caused by Pseudomonas contaminating warm water; the bacteria enter hair follicles through microabrasions, producing follicular papules and pustules that usually resolve without complications.

questions.stem: A diabetic patient presents with the plantar ulcer shown after a puncture injury. Deep infection here can extend to underlying bone. Which gram-negative organism is a classic cause of such foot infections and osteomyelitis?

explanation.summary: Pseudomonas is a well-known cause of foot infections in diabetics, especially after puncture wounds through footwear, and can seed bone and joints to cause osteomyelitis.
explanation.whyCorrect: Pseudomonas is a well-known cause of foot infections in diabetics, especially after puncture wounds through footwear, and can seed bone and joints to cause osteomyelitis.

questions.stem: An immunocompromised infant develops scattered necrotic skin lesions that began as red macules and progressed to pustules, alongside bacteremia. This necrotic skin manifestation of gram-negative sepsis is known as what?

explanation.summary: Ecthyma gangrenosum is a necrotic skin lesion seen with hematogenous dissemination of Pseudomonas; lesions favor the anogenital and axillary areas and evolve from macules to pustules to necrotic ulcers.
explanation.whyCorrect: Ecthyma gangrenosum is a necrotic skin lesion seen with hematogenous dissemination of Pseudomonas; lesions favor the anogenital and axillary areas and evolve from macules to pustules to necrotic ulcers.

questions.stem: A rock pulled from standing water is coated with the slimy green-brown layer shown. What microbial structure best explains how bacteria persist in this environment and resist clearance in patients?

choices.label: E
choices.text: A biofilm of secreted extracellular polysaccharide

explanation.summary: Pseudomonas spp. secrete extracellular polysaccharides and macromolecules to form biofilms, which shield the bacteria from the environment, predators, antibiotics, and the host immune response.
explanation.whyCorrect: Pseudomonas spp. secrete extracellular polysaccharides and macromolecules to form biofilms, which shield the bacteria from the environment, predators, antibiotics, and the host immune response.

questions.stem: This schematic contrasts a normal airway with the airway of the depicted inherited disease, in which thick mucus lines and blocks the passages. What underlying molecular defect causes the mucus abnormality shown?

choices.label: C
choices.text: A dynein arm defect in cilia

choices.label: D
choices.text: A CFTR chloride-channel mutation impairing chloride and water transport

explanation.summary: Cystic fibrosis results from a CFTR mutation that impairs chloride and water transport across epithelia, producing thick, sticky mucus that obstructs the airways and creates a niche for Pseudomonas colonization.
explanation.whyCorrect: Cystic fibrosis results from a CFTR mutation that impairs chloride and water transport across epithelia, producing thick, sticky mucus that obstructs the airways and creates a niche for Pseudomonas colonization.
